title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership · Language Clinic: Scene Evidence
summary: Menus, play, pause, death, and game over are booleans that form impossible combinations; pause leaves timers running; restart retains old projectiles and score. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the scene evidence increment in explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart.
Reset run-scoped entities, timers, score, lives, seed, and transient effects without destroying user settings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 3444 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart. A defensible solution applies Scene Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-scene-evidence — Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-game-state-scenes-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-game-state-scenes-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_game_state_scenes_3(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: asteroids-scene-evidence-1kzb3j
# Competency: asteroids-scene-evidence.
# Case 1632: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.

def evidence_asteroids_scene_evidence_1kzb3j(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1kzb3j = "1kzb3j-j3bzk1-1632"
    assert evidence_variant_1kzb3j
    evidence_axes_1kzb3j = (
        "player1kzb3j",
        "seed1632",
        "inputj3bzk1",
        "frame1kzb3j1632",
        "state16321kzb3j",
        "playtestj3bzk11632",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1kzb3j)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"

options.text: For repair the scene evidence defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Scene Evidence for explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine.
Reconstruct the conditions for Scene Evidence, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Scene Evidence artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the scene evidence defect evidence boundary for Scene Evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Scene Evidence model · Theory Scene Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Scene Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9297 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Scene Evidence model · Theory Scene Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Scene Evidence through the concrete explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Scene Evidence model · Theory Scene Evidence, keep Scene Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Scene Evidence model · Theory Scene Evidence, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Scene Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Scene Evidence model · Theory Scene Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 9297 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Scene Evidence model · Theory Scene Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: If menu-to-play works, the scene graph is tested.
